name: Castillo de la Yedra in Cotillas — circular from La Resinera
name_original: Castillo de la Yedra en Cotillas — circular desde La Resinera
region_intro: Hiking around Cotillas offers diverse natural features within the Murcia Region of Spain. The area is characterized by low-altitude relief with small elevations that gently slope towards the middle valley of the Segura River. This geological setting provides varied landscapes, including the distinctive Rambla Salada with its unique riverbed and flora, and the conglomerate cliffs of Lomas de Las Meleras. Hikers can also explore the scenic Segura River and interesting badlands formations. Best hiking trails around Cotillas The most popular hiking route is Sierra Alcaraz, Los Picarazos — circular desde Villaverde de Guadalimar, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores challenging mountain terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de la Yedra in Cotillas — circular from La Resinera, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the historic castle and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Arroyo del Tejo Spring loop from Villaverde de Guadalimar, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through spring-fed areas and riverbeds, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Cotillas is defined by unique riverine landscapes, varied geological formations, and low hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more difficult ascents. The routes in Cotillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cotillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cotillas?
A: Cotillas offers a diverse network of over 60 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, including easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging ascents through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on hikes around Cotillas?
A: Hiking around Cotillas reveals a unique blend of natural features. You'll find low-altitude relief with small elevations that gently slope towards the Segura River valley. Notable areas include the distinctive Rambla Salada with its unique riverbed and flora like tamarisk and junipers, and the conglomerate cliffs of Lomas de Las Meleras, offering panoramic viewpoints. The Segura River itself and interesting badlands formations also contribute to the varied scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cotillas?
A: Yes, Cotillas has several easy and moderate trails suitable for families. While specific family-focused routes aren't detailed, the region's low-altitude relief and varied landscapes offer accessible options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Tejo Spring loop from Villaverde de Guadalimar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.9-mile trail that leads through spring-fed areas, which could be enjoyable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cotillas?
A: Many trails in natural areas across Spain, including those around Cotillas, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary by protected area or municipality. It's always best to check local signage or official park websites for the most current rules regarding pets, particularly in areas like the Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park which is nearby.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or points of interest to see while hiking?
A: The region around Cotillas is rich in natural beauty. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4820919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Tejo Spring&lt;/a&gt;, and various waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5932006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltador Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5974287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorros Cave&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; also offer impressive sights.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cotillas?
A: Yes, Cotillas features several circular hiking routes that allow you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077277926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra in Cotillas — circular from La Resinera&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.3-mile path offering views of the historic castle. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068604452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alcaraz, Los Picarazos — circular desde Villaverde de Guadalimar&lt;/a&gt;, a more difficult 9.8-mile trail.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cotillas?
A: The hiking routes in Cotillas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from riverine landscapes to geological formations, and the well-maintained paths that make exploration enjoyable.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, out of the more than 60 trails available, approximately 13 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a pleasant introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Cotillas?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads in Cotillas may be limited, the municipality is part of the Murcia Region, which has some regional bus services. For detailed information on reaching specific starting points, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving, as many trailheads are more easily accessed by car.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Cotillas?
A: Cotillas, as a municipality, offers local amenities including cafes and bars where you can refresh yourself before or after a hike. While specific establishments directly at trailheads might be scarce, you'll find options within the town center and surrounding villages to grab a meal or a drink.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cotillas?
A: The best time to hike in Cotillas is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be quite hot in the Murcia Region, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during this season. Winters are typically mild, offering pleasant conditions for hiking, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks visible from the trails?
A: Yes, some trails offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077277926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra in Cotillas — circular from La Resinera&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the historic castle, integrating cultural interest with natural exploration. The broader Murcia Region also has a rich history, and local trails often pass through areas with historical significance.
